Lichen sclerosus 
Lichen sclerosus is a skin condition that causes itchy white patches, most commonly on the genitals. There's no cure, but treatment can help relieve the symptoms.
Symptoms of lichen sclerosus 
Lichen sclerosus affects people of all ages, including children. But it's much more common in women over 50.
People with white, brown or black skin can get lichen sclerosus.
It causes patches on the skin that are usually:
itchy white smooth or crinkled easily damaged – they may bleed or hurt if rubbed or scratched The patches can appear anywhere, but most often are on the:
area around the opening to the vagina (vulva) and anus foreskin and end of the penis Having sex can be painful because the vulva may shrink or tighten. In men, the foreskin may tighten making erections painful.
See what lichen sclerosus looks like on the vulva, foreskin and anus
